data.title: LLY Vs. NVO: Whose Oral Obesity Drug Had Better Launch Reception?
data.description:
Key points:
  • While Lilly’s Foundayo was FDA-approved earlier this month, Novo’s Wegovy pill was greenlighted by the agency in December. 
  • Lilly’s Foundayo was prescribed 3,707 times in its second week on the market, while Novo’s Wegovy pill was prescribed 18,410 times. 
  • Oral obesity pills are expected to be the next major innovation in the obesity market, doing away with needles and offering customers more flexibility and ease.

Eli Lilly and Co. (LLY) oral obesity drug Foundayo reportedly generated 3,707 prescriptions in the U.S. during its second week on the market — a noticeably softer launch than rival Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) oral Wegovy pill.

Shares of LLY closed down 4% on Friday, while NVO shares rallied nearly 7%.

Reuters reported the numbers on Friday, citing IQVIA data. The drug was prescribed 1,390 times in its debut week.

In comparison, Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk’s oral weight-loss pill, called Wegovy pill, was prescribed 18,410 times in the second week and 3,071 in the first four days.

Lilly’s drug prescriptions currently amount to just 20% of what Novo achieved in two weeks with the Wegovy pill.

Foundayo Approval

Foundayo was approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) earlier this month for use in combination with a reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity to reduce excess body weight and maintain long-term weight reduction in adults with obesity. The company started shipping the drugs on April 6.

In a late-stage trial, individuals taking the highest dose of Foundayo and who stayed on treatment lost an average of 27.3 pounds (12.4%) compared to 2.2 pounds (0.9%) with placebo. The drug also led to reductions in many markers of cardiovascular risk, including waist circumference, non-HDL cholesterol, triglycerides, and systolic blood pressure across all doses.

Oral obesity pills are expected to be the next major innovation in the obesity market, doing away with needles and offering customers more flexibility and ease. The FDA approved Novo Nordisk’s oral obesity pill in December. Novo launched it to a commendable reception in January, giving the Danish drugmaker a solid head start against its American competitor.

How Did LLY Retail Traders React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around LLY rose from ‘neutral’ to ‘bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume increased from ‘normal’ to ‘high’ levels.

A Stocktwits user termed the dip on Friday an opportunity, while noting that there is enough demand for both Novo and Lilly to dominate.

https://www.stocktwits.com/Acho5280/message/651358355

LLY has gained 3% over the past 12 months, while NYSE-listed shares of NVO fell 34%.

data.title: Faircourt Asset Management Inc. Announces April Distribution
data.description:
Toronto, April 24, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Faircourt Asset Management Inc., as Manager of the Faircourt Fund CBOE:FGX, is pleased to announce the monthly distribution payable on the Shares of the below listed Fund.

Faircourt FundsFaircourt Gold Income Corp.
Trading SymbolFGX
Distribution Amount (per share/unit) $0.034
Ex-Dividend Date April 30, 2026
Record DateApril 30, 2026
Payable DateMay 14, 2026
 

Faircourt Asset Management Inc. is the Investment Advisor for Faircourt Gold Income Corp.

This press release is not for distribution in the United States or over United States wire services.

For further information on the Faircourt Funds, please visit www.faircourtassetmgt.com or

please contact 1-800-831-0304.

You will usually pay brokerage fees to your dealer if you purchase or sell Shares of the Fund on the CBOE Canada Exchange or other alternative Canadian trading system (an “exchange”). If the Shares are purchased or sold on an exchange, investors may pay more than the current net asset value when buying Shares of the Fund and may receive less than the current net asset value when selling them.

There are ongoing fees and expenses associated with owning units of an investment fund. An investment fund must prepare disclosure documents that contain key information about the fund. You can find more detailed information about the fund in the public filings available at www.sedar.com. Investment funds are not guaranteed, their values change frequently and past performance may not be repeated.

data.title: Key facts: Dogecoin at $0.1018 Resistance; Retail Momentum, $0.0884
data.description:
• On the 4‑hour chart, Dogecoin (DOGEUSD) has five rejections at $0.1018 and pulled back toward the channel's lower quarter; current range: resistance $0.1018, support $0.0884.1
  • Dogecoin (DOGEUSD) gained momentum as retail and X-ecosystem speculation drove trading. No institutional ETF exposure noted. DOGE showed resilience and potential for further upside in recent trading.2

data.title: Key facts: XRP ETF Inflows, Institutional Interest; Governance, Outlook
data.description:
• Early April ETF inflows into XRPUSD: Canary Capital $421.86M, Bitwise $419.17M (gap $2.69M), Franklin Templeton third — ETF-level fund flows into XRP-linked products.1
  • XRPUSD ~ $1.43, below realized price ~$1.486. MVRV 0.9613, NUPL -0.0402 (Fear). Active addresses +17.7% daily, 40.8% weekly to 48,946. Daily txs 2.81M, transfer value ~$29.6B.2
  • CryptoQuant: XRP spot cumulative net demand rose to 1.39B by Apr 24 from 1.08B on Apr 2, while Binance perpetual futures showed persistent net selling — tagged a derivatives reset.3
  • BlackRock and Mastercard showed interest in XRP Ledger's built-in AMM and DEX, citing these features as usable for institutional products and potential trading/liquidity solutions for XRPUSD4
  • XRPUSD trading near $1.43; hourly chart shows a tightening triangle. Immediate resistance $1.44–$1.45, support $1.41, stronger support $1.39. Pattern hints at possible near-term ~10% move.5
  • Debate on CLARITY Act and Ripple’s push for national trust bank status shifts focus to compliant institutional use; XRP-specific speculation is being downplayed.6
  • XRPUSD saw a short-term bullish signal as the 26‑period EMA crossed above the 50‑period EMA intraday. 100‑ and 200‑period EMAs remain above price, keeping the macro EMA structure bearish.7
  • XRPUSD fell after breaking a descending triangle on the weekly chart, closing below 0.000096 BTC; pattern projects a measured target near 0.000011 BTC and weekly RSI is about 33.8
  • Analyst projects XRPUSD to reach $200 by 2027–2030 and average near $500, peaking above $650 in 2031–2035; forecasts show bridged volume rising from ~$1.2T to $18–75T.9
  • Governance vote to round down cross‑chain XRP rewards and consider XChainBridge change that could conflict with fixUniversalNumber; both affect XRP cross‑chain reward and bridge accounting.10

data.title: Key facts: Heavy BTC Accumulation, $2B ETF Inflows; Options, Risks
data.description:
• Past 30 days: long-term holders added ~303,000 BTC, strategy funds bought ~53,000 BTC, ETFs bought ~16,800 BTC, short-term holders cut positions by ~290,000 BTC.1
  • U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $2B+ net inflows over eight days, $223.2M on Thursday (BlackRock $167.5M). Weekly inflows $809M; monthly roughly $2.4B. Inflows on 10 of 11 days.234
  • Deribit April BTC options expiry: ~$8.55B notional, ~109,000 contracts. BTC ~77,900 at expiry. Max pain reported $72,000. Put-call ratio ~0.93 (52,607 calls, 52,844 puts).5
  • BTCUSD traded mostly above $75,000 this week, oscillating in a $75k–$80k range; short liquidations spiked during the rally, and strong spot demand in that band may drive price toward $84,000.6
  • BTCUSD futures 7-day basis fell from +0.465% to +0.054%; 30-day basis stayed positive, 7-day funding ~-0.00945%. BTC traded near $77,836.7
  • Coinbase Institutional said Bitcoin’s rally reflects genuine demand from ETFs and long-term holders. Reclaiming the short-term holder cost basis near $80,000 is a key market-structure level.8
  • BTCUSD last buy ~ $63,500 (Feb 6). No urgency to buy now. Watch resistance $78,324–$79,260, support $74,968–$77,250, channel near $76,400, deep support $67,500–$72,900.9
  • The summary notes that the Bitcoin ETF was approved despite regulatory opposition, while the discussion also centered on stablecoin yield and political and lobbying dynamics around crypto regulation.10
  • Analyst TheRealPlanC calls BTCUSD’s drop a mid-cycle reset, expects a return to $100,000 as expansion resumes, says big buyers accumulate 10k–30k BTC weekly, and projects a peak near 2027.11
  • BTCUSD short-term: strong bid liquidity near $76,500, but signals suggest the recent rally may be overextended and at risk of a deeper near-term pullback.12
